preview_text:
The model rates Australia as the slight favorite, assigning it a 36% win probability, while the USA's win chance stands at 32%. A draw is nearly as likely as an Australian victory, at 32%, suggesting this matchup leans competitive rather than decisive.

The three-outcome spread reflects genuine uncertainty. Australia's 4-percentage-point edge over the USA is modest; the draw probability sits within 1 point of the USA win line. The model anticipates a closely contested match where no single outcome dominates, with roughly one chance in three for each of the three results.

A stark divergence appears between model and market. Sportsbooks favor the USA at 53%, a gap of 21 percentage points above the model's 32%. The market prices Australian chances at just 21%, or 15 points below the model. Draw odds align more closely, with books at 27% versus the model at 32%. The dashboard does not adjudicate these competing signals; bettors should weigh which source commands greater confidence given their priors on team strength and recent information.
